Main Crates and Their Purposes\n\n**miroir-core** (`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/`)\n- Core business logic library\n- **Routing**: Shard assignment via rendezvous hashing (`router.rs`)\n- **Topology management**: Node health, replica groups, shard coverage (`topology.rs`)\n- **Task registry**: In-memory and Redis-backed task tracking (`task_registry.rs`)\n- **Task store**: SQLite and Redis persistence for tasks, aliases, sessions (`task_store/`)\n- **Alias system**: Index alias management for blue-green deployments (`alias.rs`)\n- **Rebalancing**: Shard migration and node management (`rebalancer.rs`, `rebalancer_worker/`)\n- **Configuration**: YAML-based config with advanced feature flags (`config.rs`, `config/advanced.rs`)\n- **Scatter-gather**: Query distribution and response merging (`scatter.rs`, `merger.rs`)\n- **Advanced features**: Hedging, session pinning, ILM, CDC, TTL, vector search, etc.\n\n**miroir-proxy** (`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/`)\n- HTTP proxy/orchestrator service\n- **Main entry point**: `main.rs` - builds UnifiedState, starts background tasks, runs Axum server\n- **Routes**: Organized by feature in `routes/` directory\n- **Middleware**: Request ID, session pinning, telemetry, CSRF, auth (`middleware.rs`)\n- **Admin endpoints**: Topology, rebalancing, node management, aliases (`routes/admin_endpoints.rs`)\n- **Client**: HTTP client for upstream Meilisearch nodes (`client.rs`)\n\n**miroir-ctl** (`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-ctl/src/`)\n- CLI tool for admin operations\n- **Commands**: Organized by feature in `commands/` directory\n- Currently has stub for alias commands (`commands/alias.rs`)\n\n### 2. File Locations for Key Components\n\n**Admin API**:\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/admin.rs` - Admin router with all `/_miroir/*` endpoints\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/admin_endpoints.rs` - Actual handler implementations\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/aliases.rs` - Alias-specific endpoints\n\n**Routing**:\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/router.rs` - Rendezvous hash routing logic\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/topology.rs` - Node and shard topology management\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/search.rs` - Search handler with routing decisions\n\n**Task Store**:\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/task_store/mod.rs` - TaskStore trait definition\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/task_store/sqlite.rs` - SQLite implementation\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/task_store/redis.rs` - Redis implementation\n\n**Metrics**:\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/middleware.rs` - Prometheus metrics implementation\n\n**Configuration**:\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/config.rs` - Main config struct\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/config/advanced.rs` - Advanced feature configs\n- `/home/coding/miroir/miroir.yaml` - Example config file\n\n### 3. Existing Patterns to Follow for Implementing Aliases\n\nThe alias system is **already fully implemented** in the codebase. Here's how it works:\n\n**Core Alias Logic** (`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/alias.rs`):\n- `Alias` struct with single/multi-target kinds\n- `AliasRegistry` for in-memory alias management with TaskStore persistence\n- Methods: `resolve()`, `flip()`, `update_multi()`, `sync_from_store()`\n\n**Admin API Endpoints** (`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/aliases.rs`):\n- `POST /_miroir/aliases/{name}` - Create alias (single or multi-target)\n- `GET /_miroir/aliases/{name}` - Get alias details with history\n- `PUT /_miroir/aliases/{name}` - Update alias (flip single or update multi)\n- `DELETE /_miroir/aliases/{name}` - Delete alias\n- `GET /_miroir/aliases` - List all aliases\n\n**Task Store Integration** (`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/task_store/mod.rs`):\n- Table 3: aliases table with operations:\n  - `create_alias()`, `get_alias()`, `flip_alias()`, `delete_alias()`, `list_aliases()`\n- Row types: `NewAlias`, `AliasRow`, `AliasHistoryEntry`\n\n**Configuration** (`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/config/advanced.rs`):\n```rust\npub struct AliasesConfig {\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub history_retention: u32,\n    pub require_target_exists: bool,\n}\n```\n\n**Metrics** (`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/middleware.rs`):\n- `miroir_alias_resolutions_total` - Counter with alias label\n- `miroir_alias_flips_total` - Counter with alias label\n\n**Resolution Pattern**:\nAliases are resolved during request routing via `AliasRegistry::resolve()` which:\n1. Checks if the input is a known alias\n2. Returns target UIDs if alias exists\n3. Returns input as-is if not an alias (treats as concrete UID)\n\n**CLI Pattern** (`/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-ctl/src/commands/alias.rs`):\n- Currently just a stub returning \"not yet implemented\"\n- Should follow pattern of other commands like `rebalance.rs`, `task.rs`, etc.\n\n### Key Design Patterns\n\n1. **UnifiedState**: Central application state combining auth, metrics, admin state\n2. **FromRef trait**: Used to extract subsystem-specific state from UnifiedState\n3. **Feature-gated metrics**: Metrics are Optional<T> and created based on config flags\n4. **TaskStore trait**: Abstraction allowing SQLite or Redis backends\n5. **Axum Router pattern**: Separate router() functions, combined with `.nest()`\n6. **Admin endpoint pattern**: `AppState::FromRef<S>` constraint for state extraction\n7. **Background tasks**: Spawned in main.rs with tokio::spawn for health checking, rebalancing, etc.\n8. **YAML config with env overrides**: Layered configuration approach\n\nThe codebase is well-structured for implementing similar features - the alias system provides an excellent template for other admin-managed resources with CRUD operations, persistence, metrics, and background synchronization."}],"totalDurationMs":60149,"totalTokens":103043,"totalToolUseCount":21,"usage":{"input_tokens":1797,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":99712,"output_tokens":1534,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"toolStats":{"readCount":17,"searchCount":3,"bashCount":1,"editFileCount":0,"linesAdded":0,"linesRemoved":0,"otherToolCount":0}}}

Aliases resolve to one or more concrete Meilisearch\n5\t//! index UIDs, supporting both single-target (writable) and multi-target\n6\t//! (read-only, used by ILM) aliases.\n7\t\n8\tuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\n9\tuse crate::task_store::{AliasRow, AliasHistoryEntry, TaskStore};\n10\tuse serde::{Deserialize, multi-target (read-only, ILM-managed).\n17\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n18\t#[serde(rename_all = \"lowercase\")]\n19\tpub enum AliasKind {\n20\t    Single,\n21\t    Multi,\n22\t}\n23\t\n24\t/// A single alias record from the task store.\n25\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n26\tpub struct Alias {\n27\t    /// Alias name (the key clients use).\n28\t    pub name: String,\n29\t    /// `single` or `multi`.\n30\t    pub kind: AliasKind,\n31\t    /// Current target UID (only set when kind=single).\n32\t    pub current_uid: Option<String>,\n33\t    /// Target UIDs as JSON array (only set when kind=multi).\n34\t    pub target_uids: Option<Vec<String>>,\n35\t    /// Generation incremented on each flip.\n36\t    pub generation: u64,\n37\t    /// Created at timestamp.\n38\t    pub created_at: u64,\n39\t    /// Last updated timestamp.\n40\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n41\t    pub updated_at: Option<u64>,\n42\t}\n43\t\n44\timpl Alias {\n45\t    /// Create a new single-target alias.\n46\t    pub fn new_single(name: String, target_uid: String) -> Self {\n47\t        let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n48\t            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n49\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n50\t            .as_secs();\n51\t        Self {\n52\t            name,\n53\t            kind: AliasKind::Single,\n54\t            current_uid: Some(target_uid),\n55\t            target_uids: None,\n56\t            generation: 0,\n57\t            created_at: now,\n58\t            updated_at: Some(now),\n59\t        }\n60\t    }\n61\t\n62\t    /// Create a new multi-target alias.\n63\t    pub fn new_multi(name: String, target_uids: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n64\t        let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n65\t            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n66\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n67\t            .as_secs();\n68\t        Self {\n69\t            name,\n70\t            kind: AliasKind::Multi,\n71\t            current_uid: None,\n72\t            target_uids: Some(target_uids),\n73\t            generation: 0,\n74\t            created_at: now,\n75\t            updated_at: Some(now),\n76\t        }\n77\t    }\n78\t\n79\t    /// Check if this alias is multi-target (read-only, ILM-managed).\n80\t    pub fn is_multi_target(&self) -> bool {\n81\t        matches!(self.kind, AliasKind::Multi)\n82\t    }\n83\t\n84\t    /// Get the effective target UIDs for this alias.\n85\t    pub fn targets(&self) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n86\t        match self.kind {\n87\t            AliasKind::Single => {\n88\t                let uid = self.current_uid.as_ref()\n89\t                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"single alias missing current_uid\".into()))?;\n90\t                Ok(vec![uid.clone()])\n91\t            }\n92\t            AliasKind::Multi => {\n93\t                let uids = self.target_uids.as_ref()\n94\t                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"multi alias missing target_uids\".into()))?;\n95\t                Ok(uids.clone())\n96\t            }\n97\t        }\n98\t    }\n99\t\n100\t    /// Flip this alias to a new target (single-target only).\n101\t    pub fn flip(&mut self, new_target: String) -> Result<()> {\n102\t        if self.kind != AliasKind::Single {\n103\t            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"cannot flip multi-target alias\".into()));\n104\t        }\n105\t        self.current_uid = Some(new_target);\n106\t        self.generation += 1;\n107\t        self.updated_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n108\t            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n109\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n110\t            .as_secs());\n111\t        Ok(())\n112\t    }\n113\t\n114\t    /// Update multi-target alias UIDs (ILM-only).\n115\t    pub fn update_targets(&mut self, new_targets: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {\n116\t        if self.kind != AliasKind::Multi {\n117\t            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"cannot update_targets on single-target alias\".into()));\n118\t        }\n119\t        self.target_uids = Some(new_targets);\n120\t        self.generation += 1;\n121\t        self.updated_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n122\t            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n123\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n124\t            .as_secs());\n125\t        Ok(())\n126\t    }\n127\t}\n128\t\n129\t/// In-memory alias registry with task-store persistence.\n130\t#[derive(Clone)]\n131\tpub struct AliasRegistry {\n132\t    aliases: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, Alias>>>,\n133\t}\n134\t\n135\timpl AliasRegistry {\n136\t    /// Create a new empty alias registry.\n137\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n138\t        Self {\n139\t            aliases: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n140\t        }\n141\t    }\n142\t\n143\t    /// Create a new alias registry and load from task store.\n144\t    pub async fn load_from_store(task_store: &dyn TaskStore) -> Result<Self> {\n145\t        let registry = Self::new();\n146\t        registry.sync_from_store(task_store).await?;\n147\t        Ok(registry)\n148\t    }\n149\t\n150\t    /// Sync aliases from the task store into memory.\n151\t    pub async fn sync_from_store(&self, task_store: &dyn TaskStore) -> Result<()> {\n152\t        let rows = task_store.list_aliases()?;\n153\t        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n154\t\n155\t        // Clear and reload from store\n156\t        aliases.clear();\n157\t        for row in rows {\n158\t            let alias = Alias {\n159\t                name: row.name.clone(),\n160\t                kind: match row.kind.as_str() {\n161\t                    \"single\" => AliasKind::Single,\n162\t                    \"multi\" => AliasKind::Multi,\n163\t                    _ => return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(format!(\"invalid alias kind: {}\", row.kind))),\n164\t                },\n165\t                current_uid: row.current_uid,\n166\t                target_uids: row.target_uids,\n167\t                generation: row.version as u64,\n168\t                created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n169\t                updated_at: None, // Task store doesn't track updated_at separately\n170\t            };\n171\t            aliases.insert(row.name, alias);\n172\t        }\n173\t\n174\t        info!(\"loaded {} aliases from task store\", aliases.len());\n175\t        Ok(())\n176\t    }\n177\t\n178\t    /// Resolve an index UID or alias name to concrete target UIDs.\n179\t    ///\n180\t    /// If `input` is not a known alias, returns it as-is (treat as concrete UID).\n181\t    pub async fn resolve(&self, input: &str) -> Vec<String> {\n182\t        let aliases = self.aliases.read().await;\n183\t        match aliases.get(input) {\n184\t            Some(alias) => alias.targets().unwrap_or_else(|_| vec![input.to_string()]),\n185\t            None => vec![input.to_string()],\n186\t        }\n187\t    }\n188\t\n189\t    /// Check if an input is an alias (vs a concrete UID).\n190\t    pub async fn is_alias(&self, input: &str) -> bool {\n191\t        self.aliases.read().await.contains_key(input)\n192\t    }\n193\t\n194\t    /// Check if an input is a multi-target alias (for write rejection).\n195\t    pub async fn is_multi_target_alias(&self, input: &str) -> bool {\n196\t        self.aliases.read().await\n197\t            .get(input)\n198\t            .map(|a| a.is_multi_target())\n199\t            .unwrap_or(false)\n200\t    }\n201\t\n202\t    /// Get a single alias by name.\n203\t    pub async fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<Alias> {\n204\t        self.aliases.read().await.get(name).cloned()\n205\t    }\n206\t\n207\t    /// List all aliases.\n208\t    pub async fn list(&self) -> Vec<Alias> {\n209\t        self.aliases.read().await.values().cloned().collect()\n210\t    }\n211\t\n212\t    /// Create or update an alias.\n213\t    pub async fn upsert(&self, alias: Alias) -> Result<()> {\n214\t        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n215\t        aliases.insert(alias.name.clone(), alias);\n216\t        Ok(())\n217\t    }\n218\t\n219\t    /// Delete an alias.\n220\t    pub async fn delete(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n221\t        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n222\t        Ok(aliases.remove(name).is_some())\n223\t    }\n224\t\n225\t    /// Flip a single-target alias atomically.\n226\t    pub async fn flip(&self, name: &str, new_target: String) -> Result<()> {\n227\t        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n228\t        let alias = aliases.get_mut(name)\n229\t            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"alias '{}' not found\", name)))?;\n230\t        alias.flip(new_target)?;\n231\t        Ok(())\n232\t    }\n233\t\n234\t    /// Update a multi-target alias (ILM use only).\n235\t    pub async fn update_multi(&self, name: &str, new_targets: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {\n236\t        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n237\t        let alias = aliases.get_mut(name)\n238\t            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"alias '{}' not found\", name)))?;\n239\t        alias.update_targets(new_targets)?;\n240\t        Ok(())\n241\t    }\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\timpl Default for AliasRegistry {\n245\t    fn default() -> Self {\n246\t        Self::new()\n247\t    }\n248\t}\n249\t\n250\t#[cfg(test)]\n251\tmod tests {\n252\t    use super::*;\n253\t\n254\t    #[test]\n255\t    fn test_new_single_alias() {\n256\t        let alias = Alias::new_single(\"products\".into(), \"products_v3\".into());\n257\t        assert_eq!(alias.name, \"products\");\n258\t        assert_eq!(alias.kind, AliasKind::Single);\n259\t        assert_eq!(alias.current_uid, Some(\"products_v3\".into()));\n260\t        assert_eq!(alias.generation, 0);\n261\t    }\n262\t\n263\t    #[test]\n264\t    fn test_new_multi_alias() {\n265\t        let alias = Alias::new_multi(\"logs\".into(), vec![\"logs-20260418\".into(), \"logs-20260417\".into()]);\n266\t        assert_eq!(alias.name, \"logs\");\n267\t        assert_eq!(alias.kind, AliasKind::Multi);\n268\t        assert_eq!(alias.target_uids, Some(vec![\"logs-20260418\".into(), \"logs-20260417\".into()]));\n269\t    }\n270\t\n271\t    #[test]\n272\t    fn test_alias_targets_single() {\n273\t        let alias = Alias::new_single(\"test\".into(), \"target_v1\".into());\n274\t        assert_eq!(alias.targets().unwrap(), vec![\"target_v1\"]);\n275\t    }\n276\t\n277\t    #[test]\n278\t    fn test_alias_targets_multi() {\n279\t        let alias = Alias::new_multi(\"test\".into(), vec![\"a\".into(), \"b\".into()]);\n280\t        assert_eq!(alias.targets().unwrap(), vec![\"a\", \"b\"]);\n281\t    }\n282\t\n283\t    #[test]\n284\t    fn test_alias_flip() {\n285\t        let mut alias = Alias::new_single(\"products\".into(), \"products_v3\".into());\n286\t        assert_eq!(alias.generation, 0);\n287\t        alias.flip(\"products_v4\".into()).unwrap();\n288\t        assert_eq!(alias.current_uid, Some(\"products_v4\".into()));\n289\t        assert_eq!(alias.generation, 1);\n290\t    }\n291\t\n292\t    #[test]\n293\t    fn test_alias_flip_multi_fails() {\n294\t        let mut alias = Alias::new_multi(\"logs\".into(), vec![\"a\".into()]);\n295\t        assert!(alias.flip(\"b\".into()).is_err());\n296\t    }\n297\t\n298\t    #[tokio::test]\n299\t    async fn test_registry_resolve_unknown() {\n300\t        let registry = AliasRegistry::new();\n301\t        assert_eq!(registry.resolve(\"concrete_index\").await, vec![\"concrete_index\"]);\n302\t    }\n303\t\n304\t    #[tokio::test]\n305\t    async fn test_registry_resolve_alias() {\n306\t        let registry = AliasRegistry::new();\n307\t        let alias = Alias::new_single(\"products\".into(), Atomic index aliases for blue-green reindexing (plan §13.7).\n//!\n//! This module implements the alias layer that allows atomic index flips\n//! without downtime. Aliases resolve to one or more concrete Meilisearch\n//! index UIDs, supporting both single-target (writable) and multi-target\n//! (read-only, used by ILM) aliases.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::task_store::{AliasRow, AliasHistoryEntry, TaskStore};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{info, warn, error};\n\n/// Alias kind: single-target (writable) or multi-target (read-only, ILM-managed).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"lowercase\")]\npub enum AliasKind {\n    Single,\n    Multi,\n}\n\n/// A single alias record from the task store.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Alias {\n    /// Alias name (the key clients use).\n    pub name: String,\n    /// `single` or `multi`.\n    pub kind: AliasKind,\n    /// Current target UID (only set when kind=single).\n    pub current_uid: Option<String>,\n    /// Target UIDs as JSON array (only set when kind=multi).\n    pub target_uids: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    /// Generation incremented on each flip.\n    pub generation: u64,\n    /// Created at timestamp.\n    pub created_at: u64,\n    /// Last updated timestamp.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub updated_at: Option<u64>,\n}\n\nimpl Alias {\n    /// Create a new single-target alias.\n    pub fn new_single(name: String, target_uid: String) -> Self {\n        let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Self {\n            name,\n            kind: AliasKind::Single,\n            current_uid: Some(target_uid),\n            target_uids: None,\n            generation: 0,\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: Some(now),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new multi-target alias.\n    pub fn new_multi(name: String, target_uids: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Self {\n            name,\n            kind: AliasKind::Multi,\n            current_uid: None,\n            target_uids: Some(target_uids),\n            generation: 0,\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: Some(now),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this alias is multi-target (read-only, ILM-managed).\n    pub fn is_multi_target(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self.kind, AliasKind::Multi)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the effective target UIDs for this alias.\n    pub fn targets(&self) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        match self.kind {\n            AliasKind::Single => {\n                let uid = self.current_uid.as_ref()\n                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"single alias missing current_uid\".into()))?;\n                Ok(vec![uid.clone()])\n            }\n            AliasKind::Multi => {\n                let uids = self.target_uids.as_ref()\n                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"multi alias missing target_uids\".into()))?;\n                Ok(uids.clone())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Flip this alias to a new target (single-target only).\n    pub fn flip(&mut self, new_target: String) -> Result<()> {\n        if self.kind != AliasKind::Single {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"cannot flip multi-target alias\".into()));\n        }\n        self.current_uid = Some(new_target);\n        self.generation += 1;\n        self.updated_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs());\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update multi-target alias UIDs (ILM-only).\n    pub fn update_targets(&mut self, new_targets: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {\n        if self.kind != AliasKind::Multi {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"cannot update_targets on single-target alias\".into()));\n        }\n        self.target_uids = Some(new_targets);\n        self.generation += 1;\n        self.updated_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs());\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// In-memory alias registry with task-store persistence.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AliasRegistry {\n    aliases: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, Alias>>>,\n}\n\nimpl AliasRegistry {\n    /// Create a new empty alias registry.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            aliases: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new alias registry and load from task store.\n    pub async fn load_from_store(task_store: &dyn TaskStore) -> Result<Self> {\n        let registry = Self::new();\n        registry.sync_from_store(task_store).await?;\n        Ok(registry)\n    }\n\n    /// Sync aliases from the task store into memory.\n    pub async fn sync_from_store(&self, task_store: &dyn TaskStore) -> Result<()> {\n        let rows = task_store.list_aliases()?;\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n\n        // Clear and reload from store\n        aliases.clear();\n        for row in rows {\n            let alias = Alias {\n                name: row.name.clone(),\n                kind: match row.kind.as_str() {\n                    \"single\" => AliasKind::Single,\n                    \"multi\" => AliasKind::Multi,\n                    _ => return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(format!(\"invalid alias kind: {}\", row.kind))),\n                },\n                current_uid: row.current_uid,\n                target_uids: row.target_uids,\n                generation: row.version as u64,\n                created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n                updated_at: None, // Task store doesn't track updated_at separately\n            };\n            aliases.insert(row.name, alias);\n        }\n\n        info!(\"loaded {} aliases from task store\", aliases.len());\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Resolve an index UID or alias name to concrete target UIDs.\n    ///\n    /// If `input` is not a known alias, returns it as-is (treat as concrete UID).\n    pub async fn resolve(&self, input: &str) -> Vec<String> {\n        let aliases = self.aliases.read().await;\n        match aliases.get(input) {\n            Some(alias) => alias.targets().unwrap_or_else(|_| vec![input.to_string()]),\n            None => vec![input.to_string()],\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if an input is an alias (vs a concrete UID).\n    pub async fn is_alias(&self, input: &str) -> bool {\n        self.aliases.read().await.contains_key(input)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if an input is a multi-target alias (for write rejection).\n    pub async fn is_multi_target_alias(&self, input: &str) -> bool {\n        self.aliases.read().await\n            .get(input)\n            .map(|a| a.is_multi_target())\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a single alias by name.\n    pub async fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<Alias> {\n        self.aliases.read().await.get(name).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// List all aliases.\n    pub async fn list(&self) -> Vec<Alias> {\n        self.aliases.read().await.values().cloned().collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Create or update an alias.\n    pub async fn upsert(&self, alias: Alias) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        aliases.insert(alias.name.clone(), alias);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Delete an alias.\n    pub async fn delete(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        Ok(aliases.remove(name).is_some())\n    }\n\n    /// Flip a single-target alias atomically.\n    pub async fn flip(&self, name: &str, new_target: String) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        let alias = aliases.get_mut(name)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"alias '{}' not found\", name)))?;\n        alias.flip(new_target)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update a multi-target alias (ILM use only).\n    pub async fn update_multi(&self, name: &str, new_targets: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        let alias = aliases.get_mut(name)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"alias '{}' not found\", name)))?;\n        alias.update_targets(new_targets)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for AliasRegistry {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_new_single_alias() {\n        let alias = Alias::new_single(\"products\".into(), \"products_v3\".into());\n        assert_eq!(alias.name, \"products\");\n        assert_eq!(alias.kind, AliasKind::Single);\n        assert_eq!(alias.current_uid, Some(\"products_v3\".into()));\n        assert_eq!(alias.generation, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_new_multi_alias() {\n        let alias = Alias::new_multi(\"logs\".into(), vec![\"logs-20260418\".into(), \"logs-20260417\".into()]);\n        assert_eq!(alias.name, \"logs\");\n        assert_eq!(alias.kind, AliasKind::Multi);\n        assert_eq!(alias.target_uids, Some(vec![\"logs-20260418\".into(), \"logs-20260417\".into()]));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alias_targets_single() {\n        let alias = Alias::new_single(\"test\".into(), \"target_v1\".into());\n        assert_eq!(alias.targets().unwrap(), vec![\"target_v1\"]);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alias_targets_multi() {\n        let alias = Alias::new_multi(\"test\".into(), vec![\"a\".into(), \"b\".into()]);\n        assert_eq!(alias.targets().unwrap(), vec![\"a\", \"b\"]);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alias_flip() {\n        let mut alias = Alias::new_single(\"products\".into(),   }\n139\t\n140\t    let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n141\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n142\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n143\t        .as_secs();\n144\t\n145\t    // Check                     name\n156\t                    ),\n157\t                }),\n158\t            ));\n159\t        }\n160\t    }\n161\t\n162\t    let new_alias = miroir_core::task_store::NewAlias };\n175\t\n176\t    task_store.create_alias(&new_alias).map_err(|e| {\n177\t        (\n178\t            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n179\t            Json(ErrorResponse {\n180\t      })?;\n351\t\n352\t        // Record alias flip metric\n353\t        state.metrics.inc_alias_flip(&name);\n354\t\n355\t        // Get updated alias\n356\t        let updated = Atomic index alias management endpoints (plan §13.7).\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{FromRef, Path, State},\n    http::StatusCode,\n    Json,\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    alias::{Alias, AliasKind},\n    config::MiroirConfig,\n    task_store::TaskStore,\n};\nuse crate::middleware::Metrics;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\n/// Alias management state.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AliasState {\n    pub config: Arc<MiroirConfig>,\n    pub task_store: Option<Arc<dyn TaskStore>>,\n    pub metrics: Metrics,\n}\n\n/// Request body for POST /_miroir/aliases.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct CreateAliasRequest {\n    /// Single target (creates single-target alias)\n    pub target: Option<String>,\n    /// Multiple targets (creates multi-target alias)\n    pub targets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n}\n\n/// Request body for PUT /_miroir/aliases/{name}.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct UpdateAliasRequest {\n    /// New target for single-target alias flip\n    pub target: Option<String>,\n    /// New targets for multi-target alias update (ILM-only)\n    pub targets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n}\n\n/// Response for GET /_miroir/aliases/{name}.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct GetAliasResponse {\n    pub name: String,\n    pub kind: String,\n    pub current_uid: Option<String>,\n    pub target_uids: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    pub version: u64,\n    pub created_at: u64,\n    pub history: Vec<AliasHistoryEntry>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct AliasHistoryEntry {\n    pub uid: String,\n    pub flipped_at: u64,\n}\n\n/// Response for LIST /_miroir/aliases.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct ListAliasesResponse {\n    pub aliases: Vec<AliasInfo>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct AliasInfo {\n    pub name: String,\n    pub kind: String,\n    pub current_uid: Option<String>,\n    pub target_uids: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    pub version: u64,\n}\n\n/// Error response for 409 conflicts.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct ErrorResponse {\n    pub code: String,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// POST /_miroir/aliases/{name} — create a new alias.\n///\n/// Request body:\n/// - Single-target: `{\"target\": \"products_v3\"}`\n/// - Multi-target: `{\"targets\": [\"logs-2026-01-01\", \"logs-2026-01-02\"]}`\n///\n/// Plan §13.7: Atomic index aliases for blue-green reindexing.\npub async fn create_alias<S>(\n    State(state): State<AliasState>,\n    Path(name): Path<String>,\n    Json(body): Json<CreateAliasRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<GetAliasResponse>, (StatusCode, Json<ErrorResponse>)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AliasState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    if !state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        return Err((\n            StatusCode::NOT_IMPLEMENTED,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"feature_disabled\".to_string(),\n                message: \"aliases feature is disabled\".to_string(),\n            }),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let task_store = state.task_store.as_ref().ok_or_else(|| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"task_store_unavailable\".to_string(),\n                message: \"task store required for aliases\".to_string(),\n            }),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // Determine alias kind from request body\n    let (kind, current_uid, target_uids) = match (&body.target, &body.targets) {\n        (Some(target), None) => (AliasKind::Single, Some(target.clone()), None),\n        (None, Some(targets)) => (AliasKind::Multi, None, Some(targets.clone())),\n        _ => {\n            return Err((\n                StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n                Json(ErrorResponse {\n                    code: \"invalid_request\".to_string(),\n                    message: \"must provide either 'target' (single) or 'targets' (multi)\".to_string(),\n                }),\n            ));\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Validate target existence if required\n    if state.config.aliases.require_target_exists {\n        // TODO: Check if target index exists in Meilisearch\n        // This would require calling the index list endpoint on each node\n    }\n\n    let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs();\n\n    // Check for conflicts with ILM-managed aliases\n    if let Ok(Some(existing)) = task_store.get_alias(&name) {\n        if existing.kind == \"multi\" {\n            // Multi-target aliases are ILM-managed and cannot be created by operators\n            return Err((\n                StatusCode::CONFLICT,\n                Json(ErrorResponse {\n                    code: \"alias_exists_ilm_managed\".to_string(),\n                    message: format!(\n                        \"alias '{}' exists and is managed by ILM policy; use ILM API to modify\",\n                        name\n                    ),\n                }),\n            ));\n        }\n    }\n\n    let new_alias = miroir_core::task_store::NewAlias {\n        name: name.clone(),\n        kind: if matches!(kind, AliasKind::Single) {\n            \"single\".to_string()\n        } else {\n            \"multi\".to_string()\n        },\n        current_uid,\n        target_uids,\n        version: 1,\n        created_at: now as i64,\n        history: vec![],\n    };\n\n    task_store.create_alias(&new_alias).map_err(|e| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"alias_creation_failed\".to_string(),\n                message: format!(\"failed to create alias: {}\", e),\n            }),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    Ok(Json(GetAliasResponse {\n        name: new_alias.name,\n        kind: new_alias.kind,\n        current_uid: new_alias.current_uid,\n        target_uids: new_alias.target_uids,\n        version: new_alias.version as u64,\n        created_at: new_alias.created_at as u64,\n        history: vec![],\n    }))\n}\n\n/// GET /_miroir/aliases/{name} — get alias details including history.\npub async fn get_alias<S>(\n    State(state): State<AliasState>,\n    Path(name): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<GetAliasResponse>, (StatusCode, Json<ErrorResponse>)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AliasState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    if !state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        return Err((\n            StatusCode::NOT_IMPLEMENTED,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"feature_disabled\".to_string(),\n                message: \"aliases feature is disabled\".to_string(),\n            }),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let task_store = state.task_store.as_ref().ok_or_else(|| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"task_store_unavailable\".to_string(),\n                message: \"task store required for aliases\".to_string(),\n            }),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let alias = task_store.get_alias(&name).map_err(|e| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"alias_lookup_failed\".to_string(),\n                message: format!(\"failed to lookup alias: {}\", e),\n            }),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    match alias {\n        Some(alias) => {\n            let history = alias.history.into_iter().map(|entry| AliasHistoryEntry {\n                uid: entry.uid,\n                flipped_at: entry.flipped_at as u64,\n            }).collect();\n\n            Ok(Json(GetAliasResponse {\n                name: alias.name,\n                kind: alias.kind,\n                current_uid: alias.current_uid,\n                target_uids: alias.target_uids,\n                version: alias.version as u64,\n                created_at: alias.created_at as u64,\n                history,\n            }))\n        }\n        None => Err((\n            StatusCode::NOT_FOUND,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"alias_not_found\".to_string(),\n                message: format!(\"alias '{}' not found\", name),\n            }),\n        )),\n    }\n}\n\n/// PUT /_miroir/aliases/{name} — update an alias (flip single or update multi).\n///\n/// Request body for single-target flip:\n/// - `{\"target\": \"products_v4\"}`\n///\n/// Request body for multi-target update (ILM-only):\n/// - `{\"targets\": [\"logs-2026-01-03\", \"logs-2026-01-02\"]}`\npub async fn update_alias<S>(\n    State(state): State<AliasState>,\n    Path(name): Path<String>,\n    Json(body): Json<UpdateAliasRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<GetAliasResponse>, (StatusCode, Json<ErrorResponse>)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AliasState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    if !state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        return Err((\n            StatusCode::NOT_IMPLEMENTED,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"feature_disabled\".to_string(),\n                message: \"aliases feature is disabled\".to_string(),\n            }),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let task_store = state.task_store.as_ref().ok_or_else(|| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"task_store_unavailable\".to_string(),\n                message: \"task store required for aliases\".to_string(),\n            }),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // Get existing alias\n    let existing = task_store.get_alias(&name).map_err(|e| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"alias_lookup_failed\".to_string(),\n                message: format!(\"failed to lookup alias: {}\", e),\n            }),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let existing = existing.ok_or_else(|| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::NOT_FOUND,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"alias_not_found\".to_string(),\n                message: format!(\"alias '{}' not found\", name),\n            }),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // Handle single-target alias flip\n    if existing.kind == \"single\" {\n        let new_target = body.target.ok_or_else(|| {\n            (\n                StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n                Json(ErrorResponse {\n                    code: \"invalid_request\".to_string(),\n                    message: \"single-target alias requires 'target' field\".to_string(),\n                }),\n            )\n        })?;\n\n        // Validate target existence if required\n        if state.config.aliases.require_target_exists {\n            // TODO: Check if target index exists in Meilisearch\n        }\n\n        // Perform the atomic flip\n        task_store.flip_alias(\n            &name,\n            &new_target,\n            state.config.aliases.history_retention as usize,\n        ).map_err(|e| {\n            (\n                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n                Json(ErrorResponse {\n                    code: \"alias_flip_failed\".to_string(),\n                    message: format!(\"failed to flip alias: {}\", e),\n                }),\n            )\n        })?;\n\n        // Record alias flip metric\n        state.metrics.inc_alias_flip(&name);\n\n        // Get updated alias\n        let updated = task_store.get_alias(&name).map_err(|e| {\n            (\n                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n                Json(ErrorResponse {\n                    code: \"alias_lookup_failed\".to_string(),\n                    message: format!(\"failed to lookup updated alias: {}\", e),\n                }),\n            )\n        })?.unwrap();\n\n        let history = updated.history.into_iter().map(|entry| AliasHistoryEntry {\n            uid: entry.uid,\n            flipped_at: entry.flipped_at as u64,\n        }).collect();\n\n        Ok(Json(GetAliasResponse {\n            name: updated.name,\n            kind: updated.kind,\n            current_uid: updated.current_uid,\n            target_uids: updated.target_uids,\n            version: updated.version as u64,\n            created_at: updated.created_at as u64,\n            history,\n        }))\n    } else {\n        // Handle multi-target alias update (ILM-only)\n        // Reject operator edits to ILM-managed multi-target aliases\n        Err((\n            StatusCode::CONFLICT,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"miroir_multi_alias_not_writable\".to_string(),\n                message: \"multi-target aliases are managed exclusively by ILM; use the ILM policy API to modify\".to_string(),\n            }),\n        ))\n    }\n}\n\n/// DELETE /_miroir/aliases/{name} — delete an alias.\npub async fn delete_alias<S>(\n    State(state): State<AliasState>,\n    Path(name): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<StatusCode, (StatusCode, Json<ErrorResponse>)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AliasState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    if !state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        return Err((\n            StatusCode::NOT_IMPLEMENTED,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"feature_disabled\".to_string(),\n                message: \"aliases feature is disabled\".to_string(),\n            }),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let task_store = state.task_store.as_ref().ok_or_else(|| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"task_store_unavailable\".to_string(),\n                message: \"task store required for aliases\".to_string(),\n            }),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let deleted = task_store.delete_alias(&name).map_err(|e| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"alias_deletion_failed\".to_string(),\n                message: format!(\"failed to delete alias: {}\", e),\n            }),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    if deleted {\n        Ok(StatusCode::NO_CONTENT)\n    } else {\n        Err((\n            StatusCode::NOT_FOUND,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"alias_not_found\".to_string(),\n                message: format!(\"alias '{}' not found\", name),\n            }),\n        ))\n    }\n}\n\n/// GET /_miroir/aliases — list all aliases.\npub async fn FromRef<S>,\n{\n    if !state.config.aliases.enabled {\n        return Err((\n            StatusCode::NOT_IMPLEMENTED,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code:             message: \"task store required for aliases\".to_string(),\n            }),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let aliases = task_store.list_aliases().map_err(|e| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            Json(ErrorResponse {\n                code: \"alias_list_failed\".to_string(),\n                message: format!(\"failed to list aliases: {}\", e),\n            }),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let alias_infos: Vec<AliasInfo> = aliases\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|alias| AliasInfo {\n            name: alias.name,\n            kind: alias.kind,\n            current_uid: alias.current_uid,\n            target_uids: alias.target_uids,\n            version: alias.version as u64,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    Ok(Json(ListAliasesResponse {\n        aliases: alias_infos,\n    }))\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use axum::body::Body;\n    use axum::http::Request;\n    use           FROM jobs WHERE id = ?1\",\n512\t                params![id],\n513\t                |row| {\n514\t                    Ok(JobRow {\n515\t                        id: row.get(0)?,\n516\t     WHERE id = ?3 AND state = 'queued'\",\n534\t            params![claimed_by, claim_expires_at, id],\n535\t        )?;\n536\t        Ok(rows > 0)\n537\t    }\n538\t\n539\t    fn fn try_acquire_leader_lease(\n584\t        &self,\n585\t        scope: &str,\n586\t        holder: &str,\n587\t        expires_at: i64,\n588\t        now_ms: i64,\n589\t    ) -> Result<bool> {\n590\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n591\t        let existing: Option<LeaderLeaseRow> = conn\n592\t            .query_row(\n593\t                \"SELECT scope, holder, expires_at FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1\",\n594\t                params![scope],\n595\t                |row| {\n596\t                    Ok(LeaderLeaseRow {\n597\t                        scope: row.get(0)?,\n598\t                        holder: row.get(1)?,\n599\t                        expires_at: row.get(2)?,\n600\t                    })\n601\t                },\n602\t            )\n603\t            .optional()?;\n604\t\n605\t        match existing {\n606\t            None => {\n607\t                conn.execute(\n608\t                    \"INSERT INTO leader_lease (scope, holder, expires_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)\",\n609\t                    params![scope, holder, expires_at],\n610\t                )?;\n611\t                Ok(true)\n612\t            }\n613\t            Some(lease) if lease.holder == holder || lease.expires_at <= now_ms => {\n614\t                let rows = conn.execute(\n615\t                    \"UPDATE leader_lease SET holder = ?1, expires_at = ?2 WHERE scope = ?3\",\n616\t                    params![holder, expires_at, scope],\n617\t                )?;\n618\t                Ok(rows > 0)\n619\t            }\n620\t            Some(_) => Ok(false),\n621\t        }\n622\t    }\n623\t\n624\t    fn renew_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str, holder: &str, expires_at: i64) -> Result<bool> {\n625\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n626\t        let rows = conn.execute(\n627\t            \"UPDATE leader_lease SET expires_at = ?1 WHERE scope = ?2 AND holder = ?3\",\n628\t            params![expires_at, scope, holder],\n629\t        )?;\n630\t        Ok(rows > 0)\n631\t    }\n632\t\n633\t    fn get_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str) -> Result<Option<LeaderLeaseRow>> {\n634\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n635\t        Ok(conn\n636\t            .query_row(\n637\t                \"SELECT scope, holder, expires_at FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1\",\n638\t doesn't support LIMIT in DELETE directly, so use a subquery\n655\t        let rows = conn.execute(\n656\t            \"DELETE FROM tasks WHERE rowid IN (\n657\t                SELECT rowid FROM tasks\n658\t                WHERE created_at < ?1 AND status IN ('succeeded', 'failed', 'canceled')\n659\t                LIMIT ?2\n660\t            )\",\n661\t            params![cutoff_ms, batch_size],\n662\t        )?;\n663\t        Ok(rows)\n664\t    }\n665\t\n666\t    fn task_count(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n667\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n668\t        let count: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tasks\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n669\t        Ok(count as u64)\n670\t    }\n671\t\n672\t    // --- Table 8: canaries ---\n673\t\n674\t    fn insert_canary_run(&self, run: &NewCanaryRun, run_history_limit: usize) -> Result<()> {\n757\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n758\t        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n759\t\n760\t        // Insert the new run\n761\t        tx.execute(\n762\t            \"INSERT INTO canary_runs (canary_id, ran_at, status, latency_ms, failed_assertions_json)\n763\t             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n764\t            params![\n765\t                run.canary_id,\n766\t                run.ran_at,\n767\t                run.status,\n768\t                run.latency_ms,\n769\t                run.failed_assertions_json,\n770\t            ],\n771\t        )?;\n772\t\n773\t        // Prune old runs to stay within the history limit\n774\t        // We want to keep only the most recent N runs (where N = run_history_limit)\n775\t        // Delete any runs that are NOT among the N most recent\n776\t        let limit = run_history_limit as i64;\n777\t        tx.execute(\n778\t            \"DELETE FROM canary_runs\n779\t             WHERE canary_id = ?1\n780\t               AND ran_at NOT IN (\n781\t                   SELECT ran_at\n782\t                   FROM canary_runs\n783\t                   WHERE canary_id = ?1\n784\t                   ORDER BY ran_at DESC\n785\t                   LIMIT ?2\n786\t               )\",\n787\t            params![run.canary_id, limit],\n788\t        )?;\n789\t\n790\t        tx.commit()?;\n791\t        Ok(())\n792\t    }\n793\t\n794\t    fn get_canary_runs(&self, canary_id: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<CanaryRunRow>> {\n795\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n796\t        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n797\t            \"SELECT canary_id, ran_at, status, latency_ms, failed_assertions_json\n798\t             FROM canary_runs\n799\t             WHERE canary_id = ?1\n800\t             ORDER BY ran_at DESC\n801\t             LIMIT ?2\",\n802\t        )?;\n803\t        let rows = stmt.query_map(params![canary_id, limit as i64], |row| {\n804\t            Ok(CanaryRunRow {\n805\t                canary_id: row.get(0)?,\n806\t                ran_at: row.get(1)?,\n807\t                status: row.get(2)?,\n808\t                latency_ms: row.get(3)?,\n809\t                failed_assertions_json: row.get(4)?,\n810\t            })\n811\t Table 7: leader_lease ---\n1491\t\n1492\t    #[test]\n1493\t    fn leader_lease_acquire_renew_steal() {\n1494\t        let store = test_store();\n1495\t\n1496\t        // First acquisition (now=0, expires=10000)\n1497\t        assert!(store\n1498\t            .try_acquire_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-a\", 10000, 0)\n1499\t            .unwrap());\n1500\t\n1501\t        // Same holder can re-acquire (now=5000, extends to 15000)\n1502\t        assert!(store\n1503\t            .try_acquire_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-a\", 15000, 5000)\n1504\t            .unwrap());\n1505\t\n1506\t        // Different holder, lease not expired — fails (now=6000, lease=15000)\n1507\t        assert!(!store\n1508\t            .try_acquire_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-b\", 20000, 6000)\n1509\t            .unwrap());\n1510\t\n1511\t        // Lease expired — different holder can steal (now=20000, lease=15000)\n1512\t        assert!(store\n1513\t            .try_acquire_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-b\", 30000, 20000)\n1514\t            .unwrap());\n1515\t\n1516\t        // Renew by current holder\n1517\t        assert!(store.renew_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-b\", 35000).unwrap());\n1518\t\n1519\t        // Wrong holder cannot renew\n1520\t        assert!(!store.renew_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\", \"pod-a\", 35000).unwrap());\n1521\t\n1522\t        // Get lease\n1523\t        let lease = store.get_leader_lease(\"reshard:idx-1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n1524\t        assert_eq!(lease.holder, \"pod-b\");\n1525\t        assert_eq!(lease.expires_at, 35000);\n1526\t    }\n1527\t\n1528\t    // --- Migration idempotency ---\n1529\t\n1530\t    #[test]\n1531\t    fn migration_is_idempotent() {\n1532\t        let store = SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap();\n1533\t        store.migrate().unwrap();\n1534\t\n1535\t        // Insert data to prove it survives re-migration\n1536\t        store\n1537\t            .insert_task(&NewTask {\n1538\t                miroir_id: \"survivor\".to_string(),\n1539\t                created_at: 1,\n1540\t                status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n1541\t                node_tasks: HashMap::new(),\n1542\t                error: None,\n1543\t                started_at: None,\n1544\t                finished_at: None,\n1545\t                index_uid: None,\n1546\t                task_type: None,\n1547\t                node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n1548\t            })\n1549\t            .unwrap();\n1550\t\n1551\t        // Run migration again — should be a no-op\n1552\t        store.migrate().unwrap();\n1553\t\n1554\t        // Data still there\n1555\t        assert!(store.get_task(\"survivor\").unwrap().is_some());\n1556\t    }\n1557\t\n1558\t    #[test]\n1559\t    fn schema_version_recorded() {\n1560\t        let store = SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap();\n1561\t        store.migrate().unwrap();\n1562\t\n1563\t        let conn = store.conn.lock().unwrap();\n1564\t        let version: i64 = conn\n1565\t            .query_row(\n1566\t                \"SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_versions\",\n1567\t                [],\n1568\t                |row| row.get(0),\n1569\t            )\n1570\t            .unwrap();\n1571\t        assert_eq!(version, registry().max_version());\n1572\t    }\n1573\t\n1574\t    // --- Schema version ahead error ---\n1575\t\n1576\t    #[test]\n1577\t    fn schema_version_ahead_fails() {\n1578\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n1579\t        let path = dir.path().join(\"test.db\");\n1580\t\n1581\t        // Create a store with current binary\n1582\t        let store = SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).unwrap();\n1583\t        store.migrate().unwrap();\n1584\t        drop(store);\n1585\t\n1586\t        // Artificially set schema version ahead of binary\n1587\t        let conn = Connection::open(&path).unwrap();\n1588\t        conn.execute(\n1589\t            \"INSERT INTO schema_versions (version, applied_at) VALUES (?1, ?2)\",\n1590\t            params![registry().max_version() + 1, now_ms()],\n1591\t        )\n1592\t        .unwrap();\n1593\t        drop(conn);\n1594\t\n1595\t        // Re-opening should fail with SchemaVersionAhead error\n1596\t        let result = SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).and_then(|s| s.migrate());\n1597\t        assert!(result.is_err());\n1598\t        match result.unwrap_err() {\n1599\t            crate::MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead {\n1600\t                store_version,\n1601\t                binary_version,\n1602\t            } => {\n1603\t                assert_eq!(store_version, registry().max_version() + 1);\n1604\t                assert_eq!(binary_version, registry().max_version());\n1605\t            }\n1606\t            _ => panic!(\"expected SchemaVersionAhead error\"),\n1607\t        }\n1608\t    }\n1609\t\n1610\t    // --- WAL mode ---\n1611\t\n1612\t    #[test]\n1613\t    fn wal_mode_enabled() {\n1614\t        let store = SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap();\n1615\t        let conn = store.conn.lock().unwrap();\n1616\t        let mode: String = conn\n1617\t            .query_row(\"PRAGMA journal_mode\", [], |row| row.get(0))\n1618\t            .unwrap();\n1619\t        assert_eq!(mode, \"memory\"); // in-memory DB uses memory mode, which is fine\n1620\t    }\n1621\t\n1622\t    #[test]\n1623\t    fn (single-process) ---\n1637\t\n1638\t    #[test]\n1639\t    fn concurrent_writes_no_deadlock() {\n1640\t        use std::sync::Arc;\n1641\t        use std::thread;\n1642\t\n1643\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n1644\t        let path = dir.path().join(\"concurrent.db\");\n1645\t        let store = Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).unwrap());\n1646\t        store.migrate().unwrap();\n1647\t\n1648\t        let mut handles = vec![];\n1649\t        for i in 0..4 {\n1650\t            let s = Arc::clone(&store);\n1651\t            handles.push(thread::spawn(move || {\n1652\t                let mut nt = HashMap::new();\n1653\t                nt.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), i as u64);\n1654\t                s.insert_task(&NewTask {\n1655\t                    miroir_id: format!(\"concurrent-{i}\"),\n1656\t                    created_at: i as i64,\n1657\t                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n1658\t                    node_tasks: nt,\n1659\t                    error: None,\n1660\t                    started_at: None,\n1661\t                    finished_at: None,\n1662\t                    index_uid: None,\n1663\t                    task_type: None,\n1664\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n1665\t                })\n1666\t                .unwrap();\n1667\t            }));\n1668\t        }\n1669\t\n1670\t        for h in handles {\n1671\t            h.join().unwrap();\n1672\t        }\n1673\t\n1674\t        // All 4 tasks should be there\n1675\t        let all = store.list_tasks(&TaskFilter::default()).unwrap();\n1676\t        assert_eq!(all.len(), 4);\n1677\t    }\n1678\t\n1679\t    // --- Table 8: canaries ---\n1680\t\n1681\t .insert_canary_run(\n1763\t                    &NewCanaryRun {\n1764\t                        canary_id: \"canary-1\".to_string(),\n1765\t                        ran_at: 1000 + i * 100,\n1766\t None\n1772\t                        },\n1773\t                    },\n1774\t                    3, // run_history_limit\n1775\t                )\n1776\t                .unwrap();\n1777\t        }\n1778\t\n1779\t        // Only the 3 most recent runs should remain\n1780\t        let runs = store.get_canary_runs(\"canary-1\", 10).unwrap();\n1781\t        assert_eq!(runs.len(), 3);\n1782\t        // Runs are ordered by ran_at DESC, so we should see runs 4, 3, 2\n1783\t        assert_eq!(runs[0].ran_at, 1400); // i=4\n1784\t        assert_eq!(runs[1].ran_at, 1300); // i=3\n1785\t parameter\n1790\t        let runs = store.get_canary_runs(\"canary-1\", 2).unwrap();\n1791\t        assert_eq!(runs.len(), 2);\n1792\t    }\n1793\t\n1794\t    #[test]\n1795\t    fn assert_eq!(session.created_at, 7000);\n2040\t        assert_eq!(session.expires_at, 17000);\n2041\t        assert!(!session.revoked);\n2042\t        assert_eq!(session.user_agent.as_deref(), assert!(store.revoke_admin_session(\"sess-admin-1\").unwrap());\n2047\t        let session = store.get_admin_session(\"sess-admin-1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n2048\t        assert!(session.revoked);\n2049\t\n2050\t        // Double revoke is idempotent (still returns true if row exists)\n2051\t        assert!(store.revoke_admin_session(\"sess-admin-1\").unwrap());\n2052\t\n2053\t        // Test session expiration cleanup\n2054\t        store\n2055\t            .insert_admin_session(&NewAdminSession {\n2056\t                session_id: \"sess-expired\".to_string(),\n2057\t                csrf_token: \"csrf-expired\".to_string(),\n2058\t                admin_key_hash: \"hash-expired\".to_string(),\n2059\t                created_at: 1000,\n2060\t                expires_at: 5000, // already expired\n2061\t                user_agent: None,\n2062\t                source_ip: None,\n2063\t            })\n2064\t            .unwrap();\n2065\t\n2066\t        let deleted = store.delete_expired_admin_sessions(10000).unwrap();\n2067\t        assert_eq!(deleted, 1);\n2068\t        assert!(store.get_admin_session(\"sess-expired\").unwrap().is_none());\n2069\t\n2070\t        // Active session should not be deleted\n2071\t        assert!(store.get_admin_session(\"sess-admin-1\").unwrap().is_some());\n2072\t    }\n2073\t\n2074\t    #[test]\n2075\t    fn admin_session_nullable_fields() {\n2076\t        let store = // --- prune_tasks ---\n2096\t\n2097\t    #[test]\n2098\t    fn prune_tasks_deletes_old_terminal_tasks() {\n2099\t        let store = test_store();\n2100\t\n2101\t        // Insert tasks with different statuses and timestamps\n2102\t        for i in 0..10 {\n2103\t            store\n2104\t                .insert_task(&NewTask {\n2105\t                    miroir_id: format!(\"task-{i}\"),\n2106\t                    created_at: i as i64 * 1000,\n2107\t                    status: match i {\n2108\t                        0..=2 => \"succeeded\",\n2109\t                        3..=5 => \"failed\",\n2110\t                        6..=7 => \"canceled\",\n2111\t                        _ => \"enqueued\", // should NOT be pruned\n2112\t                    }\n2113\t                    .to_string(),\n2114\t                    node_tasks: HashMap::new(),\n2115\t                    error: None,\n2116\t                    started_at: None,\n2117\t                    finished_at: None,\n2118\t                    index_uid: None,\n2119\t                    task_type: None,\n2120\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n2121\t                })\n2122\t                .unwrap();\n2123\t        }\n2124\t\n2125\t        // Prune tasks older than 3500ms (should delete tasks 0, 1, 2, 3)\n2126\t        let deleted = store.prune_tasks(3500, 100).unwrap();\n2127\t        assert_eq!(deleted, 4); // tasks 0, 1, 2, 3 (succeeded or failed, < 3500ms)\n2128\t\n2129\t        // Verify task-4 (failed at 4000ms) still exists\n2130\t        assert!(store.get_task(\"task-4\").unwrap().is_some());\n2131\t        // Verify task-8 (enqueued) still exists regardless of age\n2132\t        assert!(store.get_task(\"task-8\").unwrap().is_some());\n2133\t    }\n2134\t\n2135\t    // --- Property tests (proptest) ---\n2136\t\n2137\t    mod proptest_tests {\n2138\t        use resilience test ---\n2335\t\n2336\t    #[test]\n2337\t    fn task_survives_store_reopen() {\n2338\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n2339\t        let path = dir.path().join(\"resilience.db\");\n2340\t\n2341\t        // Phase 1: open, migrate, insert a task\n2342\t        {\n2343\t            let store = SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).unwrap();\n2344\t            store.migrate().unwrap();\n2345\t            let mut nt = HashMap::new();\n2346\t            nt.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 42u64);\n2347\t            store\n2348\t                .insert_task(&NewTask {\n2349\t                    miroir_id: \"survivor-task\".to_string(),\n2350\t                    created_at: 1000,\n2351\t                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n2352\t                    node_tasks: nt,\n2353\t                    error: None,\n2354\t                    started_at: None,\n2355\t                    finished_at: None,\n2356\t                    index_uid: None,\n2357\t                    task_type: None,\n2358\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n2359\t                })\n2360\t                .unwrap();\n2361\t            // Drop store — simulates pod shutdown\n2362\t        }\n2363\t\n2364\t        // Phase 2: reopen the same database file\n2365\t        {\n2366\t            let store = SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).unwrap();\n2367\t            store.migrate().unwrap();\n2368\t\n2369\t            // Task survives the close/reopen cycle\n2370\t            let task = store.get_task(\"survivor-task\").unwrap().unwrap();\n2371\t            assert_eq!(task.miroir_id, \"survivor-task\");\n2372\t            assert_eq!(task.status, \"enqueued\");\n2373\t            assert_eq!(task.node_tasks.get(\"node-0\"), Some(&42u64));\n2374\t\n2375\t            // Can continue updating the task\n2376\t            assert!(store.update_task_status(\"survivor-task\", \"processing\").unwrap());\n2377\t            assert!(store.set_task_error(\"survivor-task\", \"recovered\").unwrap());\n2378\t\n2379\t            let updated = store.get_task(\"survivor-task\").unwrap().unwrap();\n2380\t            assert_eq!(updated.status, \"processing\");\n2381\t            assert_eq!(updated.error.as_deref(), Some(\"recovered\"));\n2382\t        }\n2383\t\n2384\t        // Phase 3: reopen again and verify the update stuck\n2385\t        {\n2386\t            let store = SqliteTaskStore::open(&path).unwrap();\n2387\t            store.migrate().unwrap();\n2388\t\n2389\t            let task = store.get_task(\"survivor-task\").unwrap().unwrap();\n2390\t            assert_eq!(task.status, \"processing\");\n2391\t            assert_eq!(task.error.as_deref(), Some(\"recovered\"));\n2392\t        }\n2393\t    }\n2394\t\n2395\t    #[test]\n2396\t    fn    conn.execute_batch(\"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL; PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn run_migration(conn: &Connection) -> Result<()> {\n        // Create schema_versions first so we can query it\n        conn.execute_batch(\n            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_versions (\n                version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n                applied_at INTEGER NOT NULL\n            );\",\n        )?;\n\n        let current: Option<i64> = conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_versions\",\n                [],\n                |row| row.get(0),\n            )\n            .optional()?\n            .flatten();\n\n        let current_version = current.unwrap_or(0);\n\n        // Validate that the store version is not ahead of the binary version\n        registry().validate_version(current_version)?;\n\n        // Apply pending migrations\n        let pending = registry().pending_migrations(current_version);\n        for migration in pending {\n            conn.execute_batch(migration.sql)?;\n            conn.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO schema_versions (version, applied_at) VALUES (?1, ?2)\",\n                params![migration.version, now_ms()],\n            )?;\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 1: tasks helpers ---\n\n    fn task_row_from_row(row: &rusqlite::Row<'_>) -> ?{param_idx}\"));\n            param_values.push(Box::new(task_type.clone()));\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        if !conditions.is_empty() {\n            sql.push_str(\" WHERE \");\n            sql.push_str(&conditions.join(\" AND \"));\n        }\n        sql.push_str(\" ORDER BY created_at DESC\");\n        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" rusqlite::types::ToSql> = param_values.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(&sql)?;\n        let rows = stmt.query_map(params_refs.as_slice(), new_uid: &str, history_retention: usize) -> Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n\n        // Read current\n        let existing: Option<(String, i64, String)> = tx\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT current_uid, version, history FROM aliases WHERE name = ?1 AND kind = 'single'\",\n                params![name],\n                |row| Ok((row.get(0)?, row.get(1)?, row.get(2)?)),\n            )\n            .optional()?;\n        let Some((old_uid, old_version, history_json)) = existing else {\n            return Ok(false);\n        };\n\n        // Build new history\n        let mut history: Vec<AliasHistoryEntry> = serde_json::from_str(&history_json)?;\n        if !old_uid.is_empty() {\n            history.push(AliasHistoryEntry {\n                uid: old_uid,\n                flipped_at: now_ms(),\n            });\n        }\n        // Enforce retention bound\n        while history.len() > history_retention {\n            history.remove(0);\n        }\n\n        let new_history_json = serde_json::to_string(&history)?;\n        let new_version = old_version + 1;\n\n        let rows = tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE aliases SET current_uid = ?1, version = ?2, history = ?3 WHERE name = ?4\",\n            params![new_uid, new_version, new_history_json, name],\n        )?;\n        tx.commit()?;\n        Ok(rows > 0)\n    }\n\n    fn delete_alias(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let rows = Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let rows = conn.execute(\n            \"UPDATE jobs SET state = ?1, progress = ?2 WHERE id = ?3\",\n            params![state, progress, id],\n        )?;\n        Ok(rows > 0)\n    }\n\n    fn renew_job_claim(&self, id: &str, claim_expires_at: i64) -> Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let rows = conn.execute(\n            \"UPDATE jobs SET claim_expires_at = ?1 WHERE id = ?2 AND claimed_by IS NOT NULL\",\n            params![claim_expires_at, id],\n        )?;\n        Ok(rows > 0)\n    }\n\n    fn list_jobs_by_state(&self, state: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n            \"SELECT id, type, params, state, claimed_by, claim_expires_at, progress\n             FROM jobs WHERE state = ?1\",\n        )?;\n        let rows = stmt.query_map(params![state], |row| {\n            Ok(JobRow {\n                id: row.get(0)?,\n                type_: row.get(1)?,\n                params: row.get(2)?,\n                state: row.get(3)?,\n                claimed_by: row.get(4)?,\n                claim_expires_at: row.get(5)?,\n                progress: row.get(6)?,\n            })\n        })?;\n        let mut result = Vec::new();\n        for row in rows {\n            result.push(row?);\n        }\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 7: leader_lease ---\n\n    fn try_acquire_leader_lease(\n        &self,\n        scope: &str,\n        holder: &str,\n        expires_at: i64,\n        now_ms: i64,\n    ) -> Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let existing: Option<LeaderLeaseRow> = conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT scope, holder, expires_at FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1\",\n                params![scope],\n                |row| {\n                    Ok(LeaderLeaseRow {\n                        scope: row.get(0)?,\n                        holder: row.get(1)?,\n                        expires_at: row.get(2)?,\n                    })\n                },\n            )\n            .optional()?;\n\n        match existing {\n            None => {\n                conn.execute(\n                    \"INSERT INTO leader_lease (scope, holder, expires_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)\",\n                    params![scope, holder, expires_at],\n                )?;\n                Ok(true)\n            }\n            Some(lease) if lease.holder == holder || lease.expires_at <= now_ms => {\n                let rows = conn.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE leader_lease SET holder = ?1, expires_at = ?2 WHERE scope = ?3\",\n                    params![holder, expires_at, scope],\n                )?;\n                Ok(rows > 0)\n            }\n            Some(_) => Ok(false),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn renew_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str, holder: &str, expires_at: i64) -> Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let rows = conn.execute(\n            \"UPDATE leader_lease SET expires_at = ?1 WHERE scope = ?2 AND holder = ?3\",\n            params![expires_at, scope, holder],\n        )?;\n        Ok(rows > 0)\n    }\n\n    fn get_leader_lease(&self, scope: &str) -> Result<Option<LeaderLeaseRow>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        Ok(conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT scope, holder, expires_at FROM leader_lease WHERE scope = ?1\",\n                params![scope],\n                |row| {\n                    Ok(LeaderLeaseRow {\n                        scope: row.get(0)?,\n                        holder: row.get(1)?,\n                        expires_at: row.get(2)?,\n                    })\n                },\n            )\n            .optional()?)\n    }\n\n    // --- Tables 8-14: Feature-flagged tables ---\n\n    fn prune_tasks(&self, cutoff_ms: i64, batch_size: u32) -> Result<usize> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        // SQLite doesn't support LIMIT in DELETE directly, so use a subquery\n        let rows = conn.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM tasks WHERE rowid IN (\n                SELECT rowid FROM tasks\n                WHERE created_at < ?1 AND status IN ('succeeded', 'failed', 'canceled')\n                LIMIT ?2\n            )\",\n            params![cutoff_ms, batch_size],\n        )?;\n        Ok(rows)\n    }\n\n    fn task_count(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let count: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tasks\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        Ok(count as u64)\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 8: canaries ---\n\n    fn upsert_canary(&self, canary: &NewCanary) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        conn.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO canaries (id, name, index_uid, interval_s, query_json, assertions_json, enabled, created_at)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6, ?7, ?8)\n             ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET\n                name = ?2,\n                index_uid = ?3,\n                interval_s = ?4,\n                query_json = ?5,\n                assertions_json = ?6,\n                enabled = ?7\",\n            params![\n                canary.id,\n                canary.name,\n                canary.index_uid,\n                canary.interval_s,\n                canary.query_json,\n                canary.assertions_json,\n                canary.enabled as i64,\n                canary.created_at,\n            ],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn get_canary(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<CanaryRow>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        Ok(conn\n            .query_row(\n                \"SELECT id, name, index_uid, interval_s, query_json, assertions_json, enabled, created_at\n                 FROM canaries WHERE id = ?1\",\n                params![id],\n                |row| {\n                    Ok(CanaryRow {\n                        id: row.get(0)?,\n                        name: row.get(1)?,\n                        index_uid: row.get(2)?,\n                        interval_s: row.get(3)?,\n                        query_json: row.get(4)?,\n                        assertions_json: row.get(5)?,\n                        enabled: row.get::<_, i64>(6)? != 0,\n                        created_at: row.get(7)?,\n                    })\n                },\n            )\n            .optional()?)\n    }\n\n    fn list_canaries(&self) -> Result<Vec<CanaryRow>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n            \"SELECT id, name, index_uid, interval_s, query_json, assertions_json, enabled, created_at\n             FROM canaries\",\n        )?;\n        let rows = stmt.query_map([], |row| {\n            Ok(CanaryRow {\n                id: row.get(0)?,\n                name: row.get(1)?,\n                index_uid: row.get(2)?,\n                interval_s: row.get(3)?,\n                query_json: row.get(4)?,\n                assertions_json: row.get(5)?,\n                enabled: row.get::<_, i64>(6)? != 0,\n                created_at: row.get(7)?,\n            })\n        })?;\n        let mut result = Vec::new();\n        for row in rows {\n            result.push(row?);\n        }\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    fn delete_canary(&self, id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let rows = conn.execute(\"DELETE FROM canaries WHERE id = ?1\", params![id])?;\n        Ok(rows > 0)\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 9: canary_runs ---\n\n    fn insert_canary_run(&self, run: &NewCanaryRun, run_history_limit: usize) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n\n        // Insert the new run\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO canary_runs (canary_id, ran_at, status, latency_ms, failed_assertions_json)\n             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n            params![\n                run.canary_id,\n                run.ran_at,\n                run.status,\n                run.latency_ms,\n                run.failed_assertions_json,\n            ],\n        )?;\n\n        // Prune old runs to stay within the history limit\n        // We want to keep only the most recent N runs (where N = run_history_limit)\n        // Delete any runs that are NOT among the N most recent\n        let limit = run_history_limit as i64;\n        tx.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM canary_runs\n             WHERE canary_id = ?1\n               AND ran_at NOT IN (\n                   SELECT ran_at\n                   FROM canary_runs\n                   WHERE canary_id = ?1\n                   ORDER BY ran_at DESC\n                   LIMIT ?2\n               )\",\n            params![run.canary_id, limit],\n        )?;\n\n        tx.commit()?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn get_canary_runs(&self, canary_id: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<CanaryRunRow>> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(\n            \"SELECT canary_id, ran_at, status, latency_ms, failed_assertions_json\n             FROM canary_runs\n             WHERE canary_id = ?1\n             ORDER BY ran_at DESC\n             LIMIT ?2\",\n        )?;\n        let rows = stmt.query_map(params![canary_id, limit as i64], |row| {\n            Ok(CanaryRunRow {\n                canary_id: row.get(0)?,\n                ran_at: row.get(1)?,\n                status: row.get(2)?,\n                latency_ms: row.get(3)?,\n                failed_assertions_json: row.get(4)?,\n            })\n        })?;\n        let mut result = Vec::new();\n        for row in rows {\n            result.push(row?);\n        }\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    // --- Table 10: cdc_cursors ---\n\n    fn upsert_cdc_cursor(&self, cursor: &NewCdcCursor) -> Result<()> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        conn.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO ?3,\n                updated_at = ?4\",\n            params![\n                cursor.sink_name,\n                cursor.index_uid,\n                cursor.last_event_seq,\n                cursor.updated_at,\n            ],\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn get_cdc_cursor(&self, sink_name: &str, index_uid: &str) -> Result<Option<CdcCursorRow>> {\n        let conn =
